product_overview: LokBhasha helps users understand Marathi government documents by producing a glossary-aware canonical English rendition and then optionally localizing that English into selected Indian languages. It accepts PDFs or pasted Marathi text, highlights glossary-backed terminology, and provides on-demand explanatory and language outputs.
core_goals: Convert a Marathi government document into a clear, canonical English version that preserves official terminology.
Allow users to process documents by uploading a PDF or pasting Marathi text and immediately see side-by-side results.
Detect and surface government-specific glossary terms within the source and canonical English to explain terminology decisions.
Generate on-demand localized outputs into one or more Indian target languages without rerunning the full analysis.
Provide optional plain-language explanation and extracted key actions derived from the document for easy operational use.

features.name: Live sample run
features.description: Run a curated Marathi example through the full analysis pipeline so users can quickly see how the app extracts, detects glossary terms, and produces canonical English.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Choose the live sample to analyze -> Submit the live sample for analysis -> Wait for the loading overlay while the document is analyzed -> See canonical English, glossary context, and result panels on /result

features.name: PDF and pasted text analysis
features.description: Accept a Marathi PDF or pasted Marathi text and run extraction, glossary detection, and canonical English localization to produce a side-by-side result.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Provide Marathi input by pasting text or uploading a Marathi PDF -> Submit the analysis request -> Wait for processing to complete -> See original Marathi and canonical English side-by-side on /result
Navigate to / -> Attempt to submit an analysis request without providing text or a valid PDF -> See a validation error indicating missing or invalid input on /

features.name: On-demand follow-up outputs
features.description: Generate optional outputs such as localized Indian-language renditions, plain explanations, and extracted key actions only when explicitly requested.
user_flows: Navigate to /result -> Open the language selector and choose one or more target languages -> Request generation of the selected language outputs -> Wait for the requested outputs to appear -> Review the newly added language outputs on /result
Navigate to /result -> Request a plain explanation or extracted key actions separately -> Wait for the explanation or actions to be generated -> Review the explanation and key actions on /result

features.name: Glossary-linked result inspection
features.description: Highlight and inspect glossary-backed government terms in the source Marathi and canonical English, and surface setup and quality context for localization decisions.
user_flows: Navigate to /result -> Hover over glossary-backed terms in the Marathi or English panel -> Observe glossary matches and inline localization context -> Open the expanded context view by navigating to /result/details -> Inspect setup summary and quality checks on /result/details

features.name: Read-only glossary status (demo)
features.description: Provide a hidden, read-only glossary status page for operators and demos to inspect glossary metadata and sync status without editing the authoritative glossary.
user_flows: Navigate to /demo/glossary -> Review glossary status, source metadata, and sync timestamps

routes.description: Landing page with live sample entry, PDF upload, and pasted Marathi text analysis.
Primary result view showing original Marathi, canonical English, glossary context, and optional outputs.
Expanded detail view for localization, glossary, and quality context.
Hidden read-only glossary status page for admin and demo use.

features.name: Live sample run
features.description: Runs a curated Marathi example through the full analysis pipeline for quick demo access.
user_interactions: Click the Try live sample entry point.
Wait for the loading overlay while the document is analyzed.
Review the generated canonical English and context panels on the result page.

features.name: PDF and pasted text analysis
features.description: Accepts a Marathi PDF or pasted text and sends it through extraction, glossary detection, and canonical localization.
user_interactions: Upload a Marathi PDF file or paste Marathi text.
See validation errors if no input is provided or the upload is invalid.
View the final side-by-side result after processing completes.

features.name: On-demand follow-up outputs
features.description: Generates optional Indian language outputs, explanation text, and key actions only when explicitly requested.
user_interactions: Open the language selector and choose one or more target languages.
Click to generate selected language outputs.
Request plain explanation or key actions separately.
Review the newly added output sections without rerunning the full analysis.

features.name: Glossary-linked result inspection
features.description: Highlights government terms in the source and canonical text using the local glossary and shows setup and quality context.
user_interactions: Hover over glossary-backed terms in the Marathi or English panel.
Inspect glossary matches, localization context, setup summary, and quality checks.
Open the details page for a fuller context view.

known_limitations.issue: The hidden glossary page is read-only and does not edit the authoritative Lingo glossary.
known_limitations.impact: Operators can inspect glossary status but must manage glossary changes outside the app.

known_limitations.issue: Scanned PDF OCR depends on the extraction environment having Tesseract available.
known_limitations.impact: Image-heavy PDFs may not extract correctly in environments without OCR binaries installed.
